diagrams.title: Graphics Pipeline Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the flow of data through the programmable graphics pipeline from vertex input to pixel output
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete rendering pipeline showing both fixed-function and programmable stages, helping understand where shaders fit and how data transforms from vertices to pixels.

diagrams.title: VBO-IBO Indexed Rendering
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how VBOs and IBOs work together to render two triangles sharing an edge efficiently
elements: Visual showing Triangle 1 uses indices 0,1,2 and Triangle 2 uses indices 0,2,3
Highlight how vertices 0 and 2 are shared between both triangles
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate memory efficiency of indexed rendering - 4 vertices instead of 6, demonstrating the concept of vertex reuse through indexing.

diagrams.title: OpenGL State Machine Model
diagrams.description: Shows OpenGL as a state machine with various state variables that persist and affect subsequent operations
elements: Global State Container (Current Color, Matrix Mode, Blend Func, Depth Test, Texture Bindings)
diagrams.purpose: Help understand why state management is crucial in OpenGL - operations depend on current state, which persists until explicitly changed.
